text: The patient is positioned supine with a 12-degree anti-Trendelenburg tilt. Four 8-mm robotic ports are placed, including a supra-umbilical camera port and three additional ports. An air-sealed 12-mm assistant port is positioned in the left iliac fossa. Extensive adhesiolysis is performed using monopolar scissors and a bipolar sealing device, revealing significant adhesion formation. The liver is nearly peeled off the diaphragm, with particular difficulty freeing segment IVb due to its location at the previous transection plane of the right hemihepatectomy. Full exposure of the liver is eventually achieved.

The patient is positioned supine with a 12-degree anti-Trendelenburg tilt. Four 8-mm robotic ports are inserted, including a supra-umbilical camera port and three additional ports. An air-sealed 12-mm port for the assistant is placed in the left iliac fossa. Extensive adhesiolysis is performed using monopolar scissors and a bipolar sealing device, revealing significant adhesion formation. The liver is nearly peeled off the diaphragm, with particular difficulty freeing segment IVb due to its location at the previous transection plane of the right hemihepatectomy. Full exposure of the liver is eventually achieved.

The surgeon frees the liver hilum from adhesions to perform a Pringle maneuver using the Hohenlund technique. A Foley catheter is placed around the hepatoduodenal ligament for inflow control. Intraoperative ultrasound is used to identify the tumor, visible on the lower screen. The transection line is marked on the liver surface with monopolar scissors. A traction suture is placed using a resorbable multifilament suture and Hemolog. The Pringle maneuver is completed by applying traction on the Foley catheter and securing it with a boulder clamp.

The surgeon frees the liver hilum from adhesions to perform a Pringle maneuver using the Hohenlund technique, placing a Foley catheter around the hepatoduodenal ligament for inflow control. An intraoperative ultrasound is used to identify the tumor, visible on the lower screen. The transection line is marked on the liver surface with monopolar scissors.

The surgeon places a traction suture using a resorbable multifilament suture and Hemolog on the demarcated lesion. The Pringle maneuver is performed by applying traction on the folic catheter and securing it with a boulder clamp. The lesion is progressively resected using a robotic sealing device, which seals, cuts, and performs caliclamping to identify portal pedicles. Vessels and bile ducts are clipped with Hemologs and transected using a bipolar sealing device. Parenchymal transection continues until the tumor is fully resected. Gauzes are placed under the transected liver surface, applying gentle pressure and water to check for bleeding and biliary leaks. The specimen is placed in an endocatch bag and left in the abdomen. The gauze is removed, revealing a spot of bile, and the transected liver surface is inspected for biliary leakage. A resorbable, barred monofilament suture is applied to address the leakage.

The surgeon uses a robotic sealing device to seal and cut the liver tissue, performing caliclamping to identify portal pedicles. Vessels and bile ducts are clipped with Hemolog clips and transected using a bipolar sealing device. Parenchymal transection continues until the tumor lesion is fully resected. Gauzes are placed under the transected liver surface, applying gentle pressure and water to check for bleeding and biliary leaks. The specimen is placed in an endocatch bag and left in the abdomen. The gauze is removed, revealing a spot of bile, and the transected liver surface is inspected for biliary leakage, followed by placement of a resorbable, barbed monofilament suture.

The surgeon releases the Pringle maneuver by removing the bulldog clamp and loosening the Foley catheter, checking for hemostasis before fully removing the catheter. Surgiflo Hemostatic Matrix is applied, followed by Surgicel Fibrillar Resorbable Hemostat to ensure hemostasis. The incisional hernia is repaired with a primary closure using a barbed monofilament resorbable suture. A silicone drain is placed, and the specimen is exteriorized by enlarging the system port incision in the left iliac fossa.

Surgiflo Hemostatic Matrix is applied, followed by Surgicel Fibrillar Resorbable Hemostat to ensure hemostasis. The incisional hernia is repaired with a primary closure using a barbed monofilament resorbable suture. A silicone drain is placed due to a perioperative bile leak, and the specimen is exteriorized by enlarging the system port incision in the left iliac fossa.
